current_phase.label: Public /chat route verified and product acceptance closeout

current_milestone.label: Unified-account entry, onboarding, homepage, and one real DeepSeek round-trip are live

progress_summary: This repo remains the deployment wrapper for the official LobeHub image on the local Mac, and the public `/chat` route is again serving after a Docker Desktop outage left `127.0.0.1:3210` without a listener. The immediate incident was caused by Docker daemon unavailability, not by a missing LobeChat LaunchAgent: `com.codex.lobechat.frontend` was still running but its previous script only waited for `docker info`, so it could not recover when Docker Desktop itself was stopped. Starting Docker Desktop restored the Compose stack and `https://hernando-zhao.cn/chat/` reached the live entry path instead of returning `connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:3210`. The wrapper watch has now been hardened and published to the canonical and runtime checkouts: the release LaunchAgent sets a LaunchAgent-safe Docker CLI `PATH`, starts Docker Desktop when the daemon is unavailable, and keeps the Compose/probe loop tied to `http://127.0.0.1:3210/`. The deploy profile now also targets the actual runtime checkout `~/codex/runtime/projects/lobechat`, restarts `com.codex.lobechat.frontend`, and includes the 3210 health check so future release publishes cannot skip the watched local route verification. A follow-up runtime drift had left `deploy/.env` with blank OIDC values and `APP_URL=https://hernando-zhao.cn/chat`; this made Better Auth expose the login button while every `/api/auth/*` call returned 404. Runtime has been corrected to the canonical private env values, and the release watchdog now runs `scripts/lobehubctl.sh health` so OIDC env drift and a broken `/api/auth/sign-in/oauth2` bootstrap are detected instead of passing a shallow 3210 probe. After syncing runtime and restarting `com.codex.lobechat.frontend`, curl and an in-app browser both verified that the public route reaches the unified login entry without the 3210 connection error. Existing product decisions remain in force: `/chat` uses root-domain OIDC, LobeHub email/password login stays disabled, mounted-path custom image patches remain required, and DeepSeek remains the only provider that has been live-validated end to end.
blockers: Non-DeepSeek provider keys in `deploy/.env` remain placeholders, so Anthropic/OpenAI/Google model validation is still intentionally out of scope for this round.
The live app still emits low-priority asset noise such as missing `favicon-progress.ico` variants, but this does not block auth, onboarding, homepage delivery, or the validated DeepSeek chat path.
next_step: Optional follow-up: apply the same Docker-daemon recovery expectation to any other Docker-backed release route that still only waits for Docker instead of starting its required local runtime dependency.
